qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ExifTool argument injection in metadata write permits out-of-bounds file creation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding reaches the same metadata write route, but its demonstrated bug is newline injection in a metadata value that splits ExifTool stdin into extra arguments. The advisory's root cause is different: case-sensitive deletion of metadata keys and omission of the dangerous pseudo-tags HardLink and SymLink.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The only qualifying finding demonstrates newline-based argument injection through an ExifTool metadata value, not the advisory's case-insensitive FileName/Directory key bypass or the missing HardLink/SymLink blocklist entries.
evidence: The advisory describes pkg/modules/exiftool/exiftool.go deleting only the exact-case keys FileName and Directory and omitting HardLink and SymLink, which ExifTool still honors case-insensitively.
The qualifying finding report describes attacker-controlled newline characters in a metadata value injecting additional ExifTool arguments through the line-oriented stdin protocol.

The bundled reproduction payload is {"Title":"poc\n-Directory=/home/gotenberg\n-FileName=evohunt-proof.pdf"}, so the demonstrated exploit depends on value-level newline injection rather than bypassing metadata key filtering with alternate casings or omitted pseudo-tags.
